📋 Roles, Job Descriptions & How to Get Involved ▶
🗳 Election Cycle at a Glance
Last Election
Fall 2025
Next Election
Fall 2028
Officer Terms
3 Years
Steward Terms
1 Year (annual)
Source: Constitution Art. V & IX. Officers elected every 3rd year. Nominations from the floor at the October General Membership Meeting in election years. 3-week campaign period follows. Secret ballot, no write-ins. Stewards elected annually at each work location, overseen by an Officer or Field Rep.
🗳 How to Run for Elected Office
1. Eligibility — Must be a member in continuous good standing for ≥6 months by the date of nominations. Cannot hold more than one elected office (except Steward). No felony conviction per Landrum-Griffin Act §504.
2. Nominations — Submitted from the floor at the October General Membership Meeting in election years (next: Oct 2028). Chapter officer nominations must come from within the chapter. Local-wide officer nominations from the body as a whole. Nominations require a second.
3. Campaign — 3-week campaign period after nominations. All candidates invited to election forums. Campaign statement posted on union website and included with ballots. No financial support from non-members.
4. Election — Secret ballot conducted by independent authority appointed by the JEB. Most votes wins. No write-ins, no proxy voting.
5. Term Limits — President and Chapter Presidents: max 2 consecutive full terms, then 1-year absence required before running again. All other officers: no term limits.
6. Taking Office — New terms begin at a JEB meeting within 2 weeks of ballots being counted.
Source: Constitution Art. V, Sections 1–14
🛡 How to Become a Steward
1. Who Can Run — Any member at a work location. You represent your coworkers at your specific site.
2. Election Process — Nominated, seconded, and elected by vote of members at your work location. Elections are held annually. Overseen by an Officer or Field Rep of the Local.
3. Ratio — Max 1 steward per 10 members at a work location. Locations with multiple stewards may elect a chief steward or co-steward.
4. Training Required — Must complete introductory steward training before processing grievances. Training provided regularly; special sessions held for newly elected stewards. Advanced training also available.
5. Supervision — The JEB adopts criteria for steward performance. The President or designees supervise stewards. Stewards who don't meet criteria may be limited to duties other than grievance processing.
6. Vacancies — Unexpired terms filled by special election for the remainder of the term.
7. Next Steps — Contact your Chapter President or Internal Organizer to express interest. They'll coordinate the election at your work location.
Source: Constitution Art. IX, Sections 1–5
🏛 Elected Positions — Local-Wide Officers
Elected — Local-Wide
President
🗳 3-yr term · Max 2 consecutive
Last elected: Fall 2025 · Next: Fall 2028
Full-time paid position
Duties & Responsibilities
  • Preside over all meetings of the Local; execute Constitution and Bylaws
  • Full-time Local employee; chief negotiator for all collective bargaining agreements
  • Interview and hire all employees (subject to JEB confirmation); supervise all staff positions
  • Sign all contracts, agreements, and documents on behalf of the Local
  • Ex-officio member of all committees and all Chapter Executive Boards
  • Vote at JEB only in event of a tie or when vote changes the result
  • Convene and conduct annual Stewards Assembly
  • Submit quarterly grievance status report to each chapter's Secretary of Grievances
  • Salary cap: no elected official or staff may earn more than the highest-paid bargaining unit member
How to run: Be nominated from the floor at October General Membership Meeting in election year. Must have 6 months continuous good standing. Elected by secret ballot of entire Local membership.
Source: Constitution Art. VIII §1, Art. V §§1,5,7
Elected — Local-Wide
Treasurer
🗳 3-yr term · No term limit
Last elected: Fall 2025 · Next: Fall 2028
Full-time paid position (less than President)
Previous treasurer → Current treasurer
Duties & Responsibilities
  • Full-time Local employee; assumes President's duties if vacancy occurs
  • Responsible for all financial records, transactions, and their security
  • Ex-officio member of all committees/boards related to spending and revenue
  • Collect all dues and other revenues; suspend members owing ≥2 months dues
  • Submit written receipts/disbursements statement at each JEB meeting
  • Deposit funds in bank in name of Local Union; co-sign with President
  • Keep all membership records, application cards, admitted/suspended/expelled records
  • Present annually a proposed detailed line-item budget including Chapter Budgets
  • Forward to International Union copies of annual reports and financial statements
How to run: Same as President — nominated at October General Membership Meeting, 6 months good standing, secret ballot of entire Local membership.
Source: Constitution Art. VIII §2
Elected — Local-Wide
Recording Secretary
🗳 3-yr term · No term limit
Last elected: Fall 2025 · Next: Fall 2028
Stipend position
Previous secretary → Current secretary
Duties & Responsibilities
  • Keep correct, impartial record of proceedings at all general meetings and JEB meetings
  • Furnish minutes of JEB meetings to each member and all stewards who request them
  • Furnish committee chairpersons copies of applicable resolutions adopted by JEB
  • Notify International Secretary-Treasurer of elected officers within 15 days of election
  • Receive all official communications and correspondence (except Treasurer-addressed items)
How to run: Nominated at October General Membership Meeting, 6 months good standing, elected by secret ballot of entire Local.
Source: Constitution Art. VIII §3
Elected — Local-Wide
Trustee
🗳 3-yr term · No term limit
Last elected: Fall 2025 · Next: Fall 2028
Stipend position
Duties & Responsibilities
  • Select an auditor and oversee a Certified Audit of the Local's books
  • Annually take inventory and verify securities, investments, bank accounts, and cash funds
  • Report findings at the Annual Meeting
  • Ensure Local funds are deposited in reliable banks
  • Recommend measures to simplify Treasurer's duties and safeguard funds
  • Perform other duties assigned by the President; periodically report to JEB
How to run: Nominated at October General Membership Meeting, 6 months good standing, elected by secret ballot of entire Local.
Source: Constitution Art. VIII §4
🏢 Elected Positions — Chapter Officers
Elected — Chapter
Chapter President (Local Vice President)
🗳 3-yr term · Max 2 consecutive
Last elected: Fall 2025 · Next: Fall 2028
Stipend varies by chapter size
Duties & Responsibilities
  • Lead Chapter Executive Board meetings; conduct in accordance with parliamentary rules and Constitution
  • Attend President Advisory meetings to advise Local President on Chapter issues
  • Attend monthly Joint Executive Board meetings
  • Represent chapter at meetings with state agencies; media spokesperson for Chapter
  • Attend contract and impact bargaining sessions as appropriate
  • Manage Chapter budget; sign off on expenses
  • Ensure representation of members across regions/divisions/worksites
  • Recruit stewards/activists; develop new leaders
  • Work with CEB and staff to develop legislative priorities and chapter-wide strategies
  • Ex-officio member of all Chapter Executive Board committees
  • Testify on bills/lobby; monitor federal and state legislation
  • Build and maintain relationships with outside agencies
How to run: Nominated at October General Membership Meeting by a member of your chapter. Must have 6 months good standing. Elected by members of the chapter at large by secret ballot.
Source: Constitution Art. VIII §5–6; Union Job Descriptions
Elected — Chapter
JEB Member (Joint Executive Board)
🗳 3-yr term · No term limit
Last elected: Fall 2025 · Next: Fall 2028
Stipend position
Duties & Responsibilities
  • Attend monthly JEB meetings; approve local budget
  • Keep members updated on union events, activities, and JEB decisions
  • Recruit new members and train them as leaders
  • Promote solidarity among members; present at new staff orientations
  • Organize meetings with stewards at job sites
  • Enforce ethical responsibility, contract, and bylaws
  • Ensure members'/chapters' concerns are addressed by JEB
  • Represent the union at community events as appropriate
  • Assist with interpretation of the contract
Apportionment: 1 JEB member per 200 members (or major portion thereof), minimum 1 per chapter. Election method determined by each chapter's proposal approved by JEB.
How to run: Nominated within your chapter. Election method varies by chapter (see chapter bylaws). Must have 6 months good standing.
Source: Constitution Art. IV, VII §1; Union Job Descriptions
Elected — Chapter
Chapter Secretary of Grievances
🗳 3-yr term · No term limit
Last elected: Fall 2025 · Next: Fall 2028
Stipend position
Duties & Responsibilities
  • Process grievances for the chapter
  • Educate stewards and members in the use of the grievance procedure
  • Work jointly with Chapter Recording Secretary on communications
How to run: Nominated within your chapter at October General Membership Meeting. Must be a chapter member with 6 months good standing.
Source: Constitution Art. VIII §6
Elected — Chapter
Chapter Recording Secretary
🗳 3-yr term · No term limit
Last elected: Fall 2025 · Next: Fall 2028
Stipend position
Duties & Responsibilities
  • Develop and maintain communications between JEB, CEB, and membership
  • Keep correct, impartial account of proceedings at each CEB meeting
  • Furnish a copy of CEB minutes to each CEB member and the Local President
  • Furnish committee chairpersons copies of applicable resolutions adopted by CEB
How to run: Nominated within your chapter at October General Membership Meeting. Must be a chapter member with 6 months good standing.
Source: Constitution Art. VIII §7
Elected — Chapter
Chapter Executive Board (CEB) Member
🗳 3-yr term · No term limit
Last elected: Fall 2025 · Next: Fall 2028
Volunteer (some stipend)
Duties & Responsibilities
  • Attend Chapter Executive Board meetings; file motions, vote, approve chapter budget
  • Maintain flow of information from members to chapter and local leadership
  • Answer member email and calls; project assignments from Chapter President
  • Recruit stewards/activists; participate in contract negotiations as appropriate
  • Represent the chapter at meetings with state agencies, legislators, outside groups
  • Conduct/attend meetings at worksite; problem-solve at worksites
  • Enforce contract and bylaws; promote union issues/campaigns among membership
Minimum: Each CEB must have no fewer than 7 officers total (including President, JEB members, and other positions).
How to run: Election method determined by each chapter's approved proposal. Nominated within your chapter. Must have 6 months good standing.
Source: Constitution Art. IV; Union Job Descriptions
Elected — Chapter
Vice President / Area-Regional Rep
🗳 3-yr term · No term limit
Last elected: Fall 2025 · Next: Fall 2028
Stipend varies
Duties & Responsibilities
  • Liaison between members and management/members and union in their area/region
  • Hold regular membership meetings; organize union actions; chair committees as applicable
  • Responsible for area/regional grievances, including Step II where applicable
  • Support, advise, and recruit stewards; mentor new leaders
  • Organize area/regional labor management meetings; crisis management at worksites
  • May act as steward; chapter impact bargaining; keeper of records/files/notes
  • Advise and support Chapter President; advise members on political issues
How to run: Elected within your chapter. Structure varies — some chapters elect VPs by region (e.g., DCF Western Regional VP), others at-large. Check your chapter bylaws.
Source: Union Job Descriptions; Constitution Appendix A
🛡 Steward — Elected at Work Location
Elected — Work Location
Steward
🗳 1-yr term (annual election)
Next election: contact your Chapter President
Volunteer (no stipend from Local)
Duties & Responsibilities
  • Address worksite issues — in communication with Chapter Leadership and Field Rep
  • Write, file, argue, and follow up on grievances (after completing introductory training)
  • Welcome and orient new members; collect membership and COPE cards
  • Advisor on contract questions; enforce contract
  • Represent members at Weingarten meetings and at worksite
  • Call and conduct meetings to communicate with members
  • Share information between Local leadership and members
  • Attend and chair local labor management meetings
  • Advise members on remedial plans; assist and train other stewards
  • Participate in negotiations as appropriate (contract, mid-term)
  • Represent the union at community events as appropriate
Ratio: Max 1 steward per 10 members. Locations with multiple stewards may elect a chief steward or co-steward.
⚡ How to become a Steward:
① Tell your Chapter President or Internal Organizer you're interested
② They coordinate a nomination and election at your work location
③ You must be nominated, seconded, and elected by your coworkers
④ Complete introductory steward training (provided by the Local)
⑤ You're a steward! Annual Stewards Assembly convened by the President once per year
Vacancies? Special election fills the remainder of the term at any time.
Source: Constitution Art. IX §§1–5; Union Job Descriptions
💼 Staff Positions — Hired (Not Elected)
Staff positions are not elected — they are hired by the President, subject to confirmation by the JEB. The JEB establishes job descriptions for each staff position. Staff hired specifically for a chapter are additionally confirmed by that Chapter's Executive Board. There are no term limits; staff serve at will.

Career pipeline: Many current staff started as stewards or chapter officers. The JEB Member → Organizer path is the most common route into a paid staff role (see Career Paths section below).
Source: Constitution Art. VIII §1
Staff — Hired
Chapter Advisor
Duties & Responsibilities
  • Primary staff liaison between the Local and a specific chapter
  • Guide chapter leadership on strategy, grievances, labor-management relations
  • Coordinate organizer assignments across the chapter's worksites
  • Support chapter executive boards in meeting facilitation and agenda-setting
  • Typically a senior Internal Organizer or Field Rep with deep chapter knowledge
Not elected. Assigned by the President/Director of Field Services. To become one: build experience as an Internal Organizer and develop relationships with chapter leadership.
Source: Chapter Advisors & Internal Organizers PDF
Staff — Hired
Internal Organizer
Duties & Responsibilities
  • Assigned to specific geographic regions covering multiple worksites
  • Support stewards and chapter officers on grievances, worksite issues, and campaigns
  • Oversee steward elections at work locations
  • Facilitate member engagement, recruitment, and leadership development
  • Attend labor-management meetings; support contract bargaining
  • Provide steward training and ongoing mentoring
Not elected. Hired by the President with JEB confirmation. Common path: Steward → JEB Member/Chapter Officer → Temp Organizer → Internal Organizer.
Source: Chapter Advisors & Internal Organizers PDF; Union Job Descriptions
Staff — Hired
External Organizer
Duties & Responsibilities
  • Organize new workplaces and bargaining units into the Local
  • Lead new member campaigns, card drives, and election efforts
  • Community outreach, coalition building, and public campaigns
  • Reports to Director of External Organizing
Not elected. Hired by the President with JEB confirmation. Focused on growth — bringing new workers into the union.
Source: Union Job Descriptions
Staff — Hired
Directors & Coordinators
Current Roles (see Directors/Coordinators panels above)
  • Directors: MARC, Organizing, Political & Legislative, Admin & Governance, Communications, Finance & HR — each oversees a functional area and manages staff
  • Coordinators: Public Sector Lead, Field, Data, Private Sector, Steward Coordinator, Higher Education, Family Child Care — each coordinates organizers and strategy within a sector or function
  • Promotions from organizer ranks subject to JEB confirmation
Not elected. Hired/promoted by the President with JEB confirmation. Career progression typically: Organizer → Lead Organizer → Coordinator → Director.
Source: Constitution Art. VIII §1; Org Chart
Staff — Hired
MARC (Member Assistance Resource Center)
Duties & Responsibilities
  • Centralized support for grievances, arbitrations, and complex legal/contractual issues
  • Work with stewards and chapter officers on difficult cases across all chapters
  • Provide research, legal analysis, and representation support
  • Accessible via main office phone or marc@yourlocal.org
Not elected. MARC staff hired by President with JEB confirmation. Typically senior organizers or specialists with deep grievance/arbitration experience.
Source: Chapter Advisors & Internal Organizers PDF

How this works: Grievances → call MARC or email marc@yourlocal.org. Organizing, contracts, labor-management → contact your Internal Organizer below.
